events.label: reasoning
events.text: We need answer exactly one valid JSON object matching schema. Need assess scenario: conductor selection & sizing, manufacturing floor, observed field condition: undersized for load + length (voltage drop). Work actively in progress. Need choose practical compliant action. The work is non-compliant because undersized conductor causes voltage drop, possibly overheating. Decision must be fail if dangerous or non-compliant. Risk high or critical? Undersized for load + length: voltage drop and possible overheating, fire, equipment damage. In manufacturing floor, high. But not necessarily critical unless imminent. We'll set decision fail, risk high. Work_stage in-progress. Component_conditions installed-defective, non-compliant. percent_complete maybe 60. value could be voltage drop? Not asked numeric reading; use 0. Findings: conductor undersized for load and circuit length, voltage drop exceeds NEC recommendations. Actions: stop work, recalculate load and voltage drop, select larger conductor per NEC 310.16 and Article 210.19(A)(1) Informational Note 4? Actually NEC 310.16 ampacity. Use references NEC 310, Table 310.16, NFPA 70 NEC Article 210.19(A)(1) IN No.4 voltage drop. Need raw JSON only.

events.label: response
events.text:
{
  "decision": "fail",
  "risk": "high",
  "work_stage": "in-progress",
  "component_conditions": ["installed-defective", "non-compliant"],
  "percent_complete": 60,
  "value": 0,
  "sound_source": "",
  "confidence": 0.9,
  "event_time": 0,
  "rate": 0,
  "order": [],
  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "Conductor is undersized for the connected load and circuit run length.",
    "Voltage drop exceeds acceptable limits and may cause overheating, equipment malfunction, or fire.",
    "Installation does not meet NEC ampacity and voltage-drop requirements."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Stop installation immediately and tag out the circuit.",
    "Recalculate full-load current and total circuit length.",
    "Select a larger conductor that satisfies Table 310.16 ampacity and limits voltage drop to 3% for branch circuits / 5% total.",
    "Replace the undersized conductor before energizing.",
    "Re-inspect before returning the circuit to service."
  ],
  "rationale": "A conductor that is too small for the load and run length violates NEC ampacity and voltage-drop requirements, creating overheating and fire risk. The only acceptable journeyman practice is to size up the conductor; using a shortcut such as ignoring voltage drop or relying only on breaker size is not compliant.",
  "references": [
    "NEC 310",
    "NEC Table 310.16",
    "NEC 210.19(A)(1) Informational Note No. 4 (voltage drop)"
  ]
}
